useful_deltas.summary: Built a no-lookahead New York RTH completed-30-minute session frame and reproducible PCS/CCS/IC train-selection to untouched-holdout dual-cost lab.

Rejected the locked 8-symbol x 3-structure 7-DTE session-time proxy cycle after 24/24 rows completed, six train rows passed both cost axes, and zero passed the complete train plus untouched-holdout conjunction.

Made PCS/CCS/IC calendar DTE safe for timezone-aware intraday timestamps, applied entry lag to every entry feature, and made exits consume the date/session bucket to prevent later same-bucket reentry.
Replaced stale generated/doctrine coverage that called session-time missing with tested built-but-rejected and usable-date-density semantics, while preserving no-living-leader language.

critic_findings.finding: F1: generated coverage still said session-time slices were missing after the capability and exact-cycle reject existed.
critic_findings.rationale: Coverage machinery now reports the completed-30-minute lab as built and 0/24 rejected, and a subprocess regression test rejects the stale missing-capability phrase.

critic_findings.finding: F2: executor NEXT treated absent raw dates as the bottleneck even though raw history had about 60 dates and feature readiness left only 21 usable dates.
critic_findings.rationale: The merged and final NEXT target append-safe raw provenance plus no-lookahead usable-feature density before one locked rerun; session-frame machinery and prior-session feature tests make the distinction explicit.

critic_findings.finding: F3: axis max_lots reported generic capacity 3 while prose fixed the operating posture at one lot.
critic_findings.rationale: No row passed, no seat or intent was created, every axis/structure states the one-lot operating posture, and max one-lot loss is $224.61. Rewriting immutable evidence labels after execution would not change the decision; a future rerun may predeclare separate operating and theoretical fields.

critic_findings.finding: F4: nine holdout dates produce only one window, making dense-negative and window stress nearly vacuous.
critic_findings.rationale: Accepted as a limitation but not a claim-invalidating defect for this rejection: all six train survivors already fail untouched holdout cost-axis PnL or trade density. Current reports prohibit affirmative interpretation of sparse windows, and NEXT requires denser usable dates before retest.

critic_findings.finding: F5: concurrent RTH prose called historical b195f5fe a quality PCS despite an empty capital path and no living leader.
critic_findings.rationale: Current readiness now labels every same-day occurrence as a historical PCS reference with no living leader; tested coverage retains a none/former-reference leader hint.

data_dependencies: Append-safe provenance-recorded raw 30-minute underlying archive for BAC/F/SOFI/PLTR/TSLL/SMCI/AMD/AAPL with download/as-of metadata.
More than the current 21 usable prior-session feature dates and nine holdout dates, obtained without lookahead or holdout retuning.
Observed historical option surfaces remain required for any claim above L0; current TSLL archive is only 2/3 market dates and is provider-backtest ineligible.
next: Promote an append-safe provenance-recorded raw 30-minute underlying archive for BAC/F/SOFI/PLTR/TSLL/SMCI/AMD/AAPL, expand usable prior-session feature dates toward retained raw history without lookahead, then rerun exactly once the locked PCS/CCS/IC open/midday/late chronological dual-cost train-to-holdout specification with unchanged DNA/gates and no holdout retune; keep L0 and do not register, promote, paper, shadow, arm, or live.
